title: mollugo teretifolia
intro: Mollugo teretifolia, also known as the creeping Charlie or round-leaved carpetweed, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Molluginaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Mollugo teretifolia include treating fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as relieving symptoms of respiratory issues like bronchitis and coughs. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water.
